blueprints.title: Coding Bugfix (Safe First)
blueprints.description: Read-first plan, then guarded execution with explicit approval checkpoints.

intent_contract.user_intent: Fix the root issue with minimal risk and clear validation evidence.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Root cause fixed, acceptance checks passed, and rollback guidance documented.
spirit_constraints: Solve the actual problem, not only the nearest symptom.
Do not stop at metric pass if required verification evidence is missing.

phases.exit_criteria: Root cause hypothesis with affected files.
Patch plan and tests listed.
Patch applied in minimal steps.
Smoke checks pass.

blueprints.title: NixOS Service Hardening
blueprints.description: Incremental hardening flow with rollback markers and runtime checks.

intent_contract.user_intent: Harden service configuration while preserving runtime stability and rollback safety.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Declarative hardening landed, services healthy, and rollback path verified.
spirit_constraints: Prefer declarative policy controls over ad-hoc runtime fixes.
Address root configuration ownership and dependency order, not just immediate restart failures.

phases.exit_criteria: Current module and option owners identified.
Changes and rollback command drafted.
Nix module changes applied.
No boot/shutdown regression signals.

blueprints.title: PRSI Pessimistic Recursive Improvement
blueprints.description: One-change-per-cycle self-improvement loop with pessimistic validation, explicit rollback, and confidence-gated progression.

intent_contract.user_intent: Continuously improve reliability, security, eval quality, and efficiency under pessimistic assumptions with strict safety gates.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Exactly one reversible change is applied, required gates pass, and cycle artifacts capture outcomes and counterfactual risks.
spirit_constraints: Assume hidden regressions and benchmark contamination until disproven with evidence.
Reject gains that cannot survive counterfactual analysis or reproducibility checks.
Prefer declarative-first controls and explicit rollback paths.
anti_goals: metric-only improvement claims without confidence and regression evidence

phases.exit_criteria: Objective, hypothesis, acceptance checks, and blast radius are explicit.
Top bottleneck selected via impact x confidence x reversibility.
Single change proposal includes files, KPI target, side effects, and rollback command.
Minimal patch applied and command evidence captured.
Required pessimistic gates pass or change is auto-reverted.
Outcome artifacts include KPI delta, confidence, taxonomy, and counterfactual note.
Next action queued as proceed, targeted-eval, or quarantine/escalate.

blueprints.title: Long-Running Context Offload
blueprints.description: Keep remote runs lean by persisting decisions and history in the harness, recalling compact context on demand, and compacting often.

intent_contract.user_intent: Reduce remote token consumption on long-running work by using the local harness as the primary memory and retrieval surface.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: A harness-native context loop is in place with compact prompts, local recall path, validation evidence, and no performance regression note left unexamined.
spirit_constraints: Prefer harness memory recall, progressive context, and session state before resending old prompt history.
Compact often and preserve only durable decisions, blockers, evidence, and next actions.
Reuse existing cards, memory stores, workflow sessions, and retrieval endpoints before inventing new persistence paths.
Re-measure latency and retrieval quality when widening local context so token savings do not hide performance regressions.
anti_goals: adding a second memory system without exhausting existing harness surfaces
replaying full prompt transcripts to remote models when harness recall could serve the same purpose

phases.exit_criteria: Existing harness memory, session, and context surfaces are mapped before new storage is proposed.
A retrieval-first, compaction-aware integration plan is defined with acceptance checks.
Minimal integration changes land on existing bootstrap, workflow, or memory surfaces.
Validation confirms context can be recalled compactly and the operator has a clear rollback path.

blueprints.title: Repository Refactor (Guarded)
blueprints.description: Structured refactor flow with explicit invariants, reviewer gate, and regression evidence.

intent_contract.user_intent: Refactor repository structure or code organization without changing intended behavior unless explicitly requested.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Refactor landed with preserved behavior, validation evidence captured, and rollback guidance documented.
spirit_constraints: Preserve user-visible behavior unless the change request explicitly includes behavior changes.
Keep edits scoped and justify any broadened blast radius with concrete evidence.

phases.exit_criteria: Current invariants, coupling points, and affected paths are identified.
Refactor steps, invariants, and validation matrix are explicit.
Minimal reversible refactor slices are applied.
Regression checks and acceptance evidence pass.

blueprints.title: Deploy / Rollback Safe Ops
blueprints.description: Deployment-oriented workflow with explicit rollback readiness and live verification.

intent_contract.user_intent: Apply deployment-related changes or runtime actions while preserving a clear rollback path and live health verification.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Deploy or rollback completed, runtime health verified, and rollback notes updated with exact commands.
spirit_constraints: Prefer declarative activation paths over ad hoc restarts when both are viable.
Treat live health verification as mandatory, not optional follow-up.
anti_goals: runtime drift hidden behind imperative fixes
claiming deploy success before service readiness checks

phases.exit_criteria: Target services, activation path, and rollback markers are explicit.
Deploy, verify, and rollback steps are listed.
Deploy or rollback command sequence is completed.
Live health, capability, and acceptance checks pass.

blueprints.title: Continue / Editor Rescue
blueprints.description: Recover Continue or editor-agent failures through bounded diagnostics and handoff verification.

intent_contract.user_intent: Diagnose and recover Continue/editor-agent failures with explicit runtime evidence and safe handoff points.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Failure mode categorized, bounded recovery applied or blocked with evidence, and editor path re-smoked.
spirit_constraints: Diagnose the failing layer before changing prompts, model aliases, or editor settings.
Keep editor rescue bounded to reproducible runtime checks and explicit configuration diffs.
anti_goals: declaring fixed without re-smoking the editor path

phases.exit_criteria: Failure layer and affected surfaces are classified.
Recovery steps and verification smokes are explicit.
Bounded recovery steps are applied.
Continue/editor path is re-smoked and categorized.

blueprints.title: Bounded Research / Retrieval Review
blueprints.description: Gather bounded source-backed research through approved workflows and require reviewer-visible evidence plus stop conditions.

intent_contract.user_intent: Collect, summarize, or compare source-bounded research without drifting into open-ended scraping or unreviewed synthesis.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Approved sources queried, evidence captured, output schema filled, and stop conditions or follow-up gaps documented.
spirit_constraints: Prefer approved source packs, retrieval, and browser fallback policy before ad hoc browsing.
Keep outputs evidence-backed and stop when the requested schema is filled.
anti_goals: open-ended browsing without schema or stop condition
treating weak extraction as good enough without fallback classification

phases.exit_criteria: Subject, source bounds, and output schema are explicit.
Research steps, source pack, and stop condition are listed.
Bounded retrieval or research collection is completed or classified.
Evidence and summary are reviewed against the requested schema.

blueprints.title: Remote Reasoning Escalation
blueprints.description: Escalate to remote reasoning only after local context capture, explicit constraints, and reviewer-visible output shape.

intent_contract.user_intent: Use remote reasoning only when the task benefits from deeper synthesis and the local harness already captured the necessary context and constraints.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Escalation justification, bounded remote result, local review notes, and follow-up validation path are documented.
spirit_constraints: Prefer local retrieval, hints, and memory before paying remote reasoning cost.
Require explicit artifact shape and salvage any useful partial output before retry.
anti_goals: remote escalation without local context capture
treating provider rejection as final analysis

phases.exit_criteria: Local context, constraints, and escalation rationale are explicit.
Remote task envelope and expected artifact are defined.
Bounded remote reasoning call completed or classified.
Remote output is locally reviewed and next action is chosen.

blueprints.title: Unified Lifecycle Orchestration (Standard)
blueprints.description: Full 8-phase lifecycle for any user prompt: INTAKE→DISCOVER→PRD→PLAN→ASSIGN→DELEGATE→VALIDATE→COMMIT. Use for all standard and complex tasks. Entry via POST /agent/intake.

intent_contract.user_intent: Execute the task through the full lifecycle with appropriate discovery, planning, and validation.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: All lifecycle phases passed, commit recorded, aq-qa 0 failures.
spirit_constraints: All lifecycle phases must complete in order — never skip VALIDATE before COMMIT.
Context passed between phases must be pruned to phase-relevant keys only, never full session history.

notes: Use agent_intake MCP tool to start. Returns session_id for tracking.
Context pruning is automatic — each phase receives only relevant previous outputs.
Simple tasks (typo fixes, renames) auto-skip DISCOVER+PRD and enter at PLAN.
Domain routing is automatic after PLAN phase — no manual team selection required.

phases.exit_criteria: Session created, complexity detected, domain classified, caller normalized.
codebase_summary, existing_plans, health_status recorded as structured outputs.
prd_scope and acceptance_checks defined and recorded.
Phased plan with tool_assignments per phase. domain_hints confirmed.
agent_assignments and team_routing resolved — no hardcoded model IDs.
sub_agent_summaries and artifacts_created recorded.
validation_passed=true, qa_score >= threshold, no syntax errors.
commit_sha, files_changed, commit_message recorded. Session DONE.

blueprints.title: Simple Task (Direct Execution)
blueprints.description: For trivial tasks (typo fix, rename, version bump). Skips DISCOVER+PRD and enters at PLAN. Full validation still required before commit.

intent_contract.user_intent: Apply a minimal targeted change with full validation.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Change applied, syntax validated, commit recorded.
spirit_constraints: Apply the narrowest targeted change — do not expand scope into refactoring or discovery.
Validation is mandatory even for trivial tasks before commit.

notes: Pass complexity=simple to agent_intake to auto-select this sequence.
DISCOVER and PRD phases are skipped. Sequence: INTAKE→PLAN→ASSIGN→DELEGATE→VALIDATE→COMMIT.

phases.exit_criteria: Session created with complexity=simple.
Minimal plan with targeted change described.
Change implemented and recorded.
Syntax and basic validation passed.
Commit recorded. Session DONE.

blueprints.title: Domain-Delegated Execution (Post-Planning)
blueprints.description: For tasks where PRD+planning are already complete. Enters at ASSIGN and routes to the domain-specific agent team. Used by the lifecycle FSM DELEGATE phase.

intent_contract.user_intent: Execute a domain-specific implementation slice with appropriate tools and validation.
intent_contract.definition_of_done: Domain slice complete, artifacts created, validation passed.
spirit_constraints: Sub-agents execute only their assigned domain slice — do not re-scope goals or route other agents.
Context injection is pruned to domain-relevant outputs only — never inject full session history.

notes: Sub-agents must not re-scope goals, re-route other agents, or finalize acceptance.
Domain routing: nixos→nix-tools, python→implementer, security→auditor, trading→tradingagents, design→impeccable.
Context injected is PRUNED to domain-relevant outputs only.

phases.exit_criteria: agent_assignments confirmed from capability registry.
Domain slice executed, sub_agent_summaries recorded.
Domain-specific validation gates passed.
Commit recorded.
